title: Concrete Hydration Exotherm and Thermal Gradient Limits
content: Portland cement hydration is strongly exothermic; in mass concrete (sections >0.5 m), core temperatures can exceed 70 °C within 12–36 hours. ACI 305R requires the core-to-surface temperature differential ΔT ≤ 20 °C to prevent thermal cracking from restrained differential expansion. Active water cooling via embedded pipes or surface cooling blankets is used to manage the exotherm and maintain uniform temperature gradients.
